xmmc

Performs the following operations: d0=s0*s1, d1=s2*s3, d2= (r0.a>0.5) ? s2*s3 : s0*s1.

If r0.a is greater than 0.5, d2 receives the value of s0*s1. Otherwise, it receives the value of s2*s3. The source registers and modifiers along with the destination registers and instruction modifiers are identical to the xmma instruction.

xmmc d0,d1,d2, s0,s1,s2,s3

Registers

d0,d1,d2

The destination registers d0, d1, and d2 can be selected from the following set:

RegisterDescription
r0Temporary register 0
r1Temporary register 1
t0Texture register 0
t1Texture register 1
t2Texture register 2
t3Texture register 3
v0Vertex color register 0
v1Vertex color register 1
discardDiscard result

Each of the registers d0, d1, and d2 must be unique. The only exception is that one or more of these destination registers can be discard, indicating that the result is to be discarded.

This instruction may be modified to specify the scale and bias applied to the outputs according to the following table:

ModifierDescription
_biasSubtracts 0.5 from outputs
_x2Scales outputs by 2
_bx2Subtracts 0.5 from outputs and scales by 2
_x4Scales outputs by 4
_d2Divides outputs by 2

Note   The _x2, _x4, and _d2 modifiers are applied directly to the instruction and not to a particular register.

s0,s1,s2,s3

The source registers s0, s1, s2, and s3 can be selected from the following set:

RegisterDescription
r0Temporary register 0
r1Temporary register 1
t0Texture register 0
t1Texture register 1
t2Texture register 2
t3Texture register 3
v0Vertex color register 0
v1Vertex color register 1
c(0-15)Constant registers 0 through 15
fog.rgbFog color
zeroConstant 0

Each of the source registers may be modified using the modifiers in the tables below. An optional channel modifier (.a or .b) may be specified along with another modifier from the second table. In the table below, X represents a register:

ModifierDescription
X.aReplicate alpha channel to all channels
X.bReplicate blue channel to the alpha channel

One of the following modifiers may also be specified to adjust the range of the value in the register. In the table below, Y represents a register with an optional channel modifier:

ModifierDescription
Y(no modifier, Y is passed without alteration)
Y_satClamps negative Y to 0
-YNegate Y
1-YComplements Y (1.0-Y)
Y_bx2Shifts range from [0..1] to [-1..1] (2*(Y-0.5))
Y_biasClamps negative Y to 0 and then subtracts 0.5
-Y_biasClamps negative Y to 0, subtracts 0.5, and then negates
-Y_bx2Shifts range from [0..1] to [-1..1] and then negates
